messages.content: EU-funded research aims to secure effective industrial human-robot collaboration through novel human-centred design and smart mechatronic systems. This includes soft robotics, advanced machines capable of handling complex materials in industrial production environments, and novel artificial intelligence technologies that enable new levels of autonomy, navigation, and cognitive perception by factory robots. - reprhase this into shorter sentences

EU-funded research aims to secure effective industrial human-robot collaboration. It focuses on human-centred design and smart mechatronic systems. Soft robotics and advanced machines are employed to handle complex materials in industrial production environments. Additionally, novel artificial intelligence technologies enable factory robots to achieve new levels of autonomy, navigation, and cognitive perception.

Collaborating with customers to better understand their needs also represents an important step towards a more resilient and sustainable European industry. Research supporting customer-driven manufacturing can lead to more efficient and productive manufacturing processes via innovative instruments such as collaborative Manufacturing Demonstration Facilities.  - rephrase this

To promote a resilient and sustainable European industry, collaboration with customers to understand their needs is crucial. Research in customer-driven manufacturing can enhance manufacturing processes and productivity. This can be achieved through the use of innovative tools like collaborative Manufacturing Demonstration Facilities.

Advanced AI systems, customer-integrated manufacturing, and collaborative robotics are all technological challenges implemented by the Horizon 2020 European Partnership (Factories of the Future), a EUR 1.8 billion public-private partnership for advanced manufacturing research and innovation.  - rephrase this

The Horizon 2020 European Partnership, known as Factories of the Future, addresses technological challenges through the implementation of advanced AI systems, customer-integrated manufacturing, and collaborative robotics. With a budget of EUR 1.8 billion, this public-private partnership focuses on advancing manufacturing research and innovation.

Advanced AI systems, customer-integrated manufacturing, and collaborative robotics are all technological challenges implemented by the Horizon 2020 European Partnership (Factories of the Future), a EUR 1.8 billion public-private partnership for advanced manufacturing research and innovation. - rephrase this

The Horizon 2020 European Partnership (Factories of the Future) is dedicated to advanced manufacturing research and innovation. It tackles technological challenges such as advanced AI systems, customer-integrated manufacturing, and collaborative robotics. This public-private partnership boasts a budget of EUR 1.8 billion.
The partnership continues under Horizon Europe, as Made in Europe (MiE).  - rephrase this
The partnership, known as Made in Europe (MiE), carries on its efforts under the umbrella of Horizon Europe.

The projects featured in this Result Pack highlight examples of human-robot collaboration and customer-driven manufacturing that are already giving rise to safer and more skilled roles for those working in manufacturing, better economics for the industry, and a production tailored to the needs of citizens in the 21st century.  - rephrase this

The projects showcased in this Result Pack demonstrate how human-robot collaboration and customer-driven manufacturing are leading to safer and more skilled roles for manufacturing workers. These initiatives also contribute to improved industry economics and enable production that aligns with the needs of citizens in the 21st century.
Want to feel healthier and happier?  - rephrase this
Looking to enhance your well-being and happiness?
Regularly saying hi to others in your neighbourhood will pay you back in ways you never thought of.  - rephrase this
Engaging in regular greetings with your neighbors can have unexpected benefits for you.

The https://news.gallup.com/poll/246200/gallup-national-health-index-work.aspx (National Health and Well-Being Index), a poll conducted by United States (US)-based global analytics and advice firm Gallup, found that adults who often greet multiple neighbours report increased well-being compared to those who greet less.  - rephrase this

According to the Gallup National Health and Well-Being Index, a survey conducted by the global analytics and advice firm Gallup, adults who frequently greet multiple neighbors experience higher levels of well-being in comparison to those who greet fewer neighbors.

And what about the frequency? “We leave that up to the respondent to interpret, but our intent was for this to mean all the people to whom you greet whenever you see them,” Witters elaborated. “Some days might be zero, some days might be every one of them.”  - rephrase this

Regarding the frequency of greetings, the Gallup survey allows respondents to interpret it according to their own understanding. The intention behind the question is to encompass all the individuals whom the respondent greets whenever they encounter them. The frequency may vary from zero on some days to greeting every single neighbor on others, as clarified by Witters from Gallup.

And what about the frequency? “We leave that up to the respondent to interpret, but our intent was for this to mean all the people to whom you greet whenever you see them,” Witters elaborated. “Some days might be zero, some days might be every one of them.” - rephrase this

When it comes to the frequency of greetings, Gallup's survey allows respondents to interpret it themselves. The intention is to include all the individuals whom respondents greet whenever they come across them. As explained by Witters, some days the frequency could be zero, while on other days it could be every single person they encounter.
